Record-001: Source is Lemma lem.det.minors-diag about diagonal matrices. It has two parts: (a) det(sub_P^P D) = ∏_{i∈P} d_i, (b) if P≠Q and |P|=|Q| then det(sub_P^Q D)=0. The Lean prefix context includes definitions like submatrixOfFinset, sub[U,V] A, submatrixDet, etc. Also there is a theorem det_add_sum that uses submatrixDet. The target likely formalizes this lemma. We need to sketch the formalization.

Formalization sketch: Use the local notation sub[U,V] A for submatrixOfFinset. For part (a), we need to show that the submatrix of a diagonal matrix D with rows and columns indexed by the same set P is diagonal with entries d_i for i∈P. Then its determinant is product of those entries. For part (b), if P≠Q but same cardinality, there exists i∈P\Q, then the row corresponding to i in sub[P,Q] D is zero, so determinant zero.

We need to use the local definitions: submatrixOfFinset, submatrixDet? Actually submatrixDet is defined as (A.submatrix (P.orderEmbOfFin rfl) (Q.orderEmbOfFin (h ▸ rfl))).det, which is similar to sub[P,Q] A but with an explicit cardinality equality proof. The source uses sub_P^Q D, which is likely sub[P,Q] D. So we can use sub[P,Q] D.

We need to know the type of D: it's a diagonal matrix. In the source, D is defined as (d_i δ_{i=j}). In Lean, we can define D as Matrix.diagonal d where d : Fin n → R. But the source uses a list d_1,...,d_n. So we can have a function d : Fin n → R. Then D = Matrix.diagonal d.

We need to compute sub[P,P] (Matrix.diagonal d). The submatrixOfFinset uses orderEmbOfFin, so the resulting matrix is also diagonal? We can prove that sub[P,P] (diagonal d) = diagonal (fun i : Fin P.card => d (P.orderEmbOfFin rfl i)). Then its determinant is product over i in P of d_i.

Alternatively, we can use the existing lemma about determinant of diagonal matrix: Matrix.det_diagonal. But we need to apply it to the submatrix.

We also need to handle the case when P≠Q but same cardinality. We need to show that there exists a row index i in P such that the row is zero. Since D is diagonal, the only nonzero entries are on the diagonal. In sub[P,Q] D, the entry at (i,j) is D (P.orderEmbOfFin rfl i) (Q.orderEmbOfFin rfl j). This is nonzero only if the row index equals column index. But since P≠Q, there exists some element in P not in Q. For that row, all column indices are in Q, so the row index is not equal to any column index, so all entries are zero. So determinant zero.

We need to formalize this.

]

- proof_notes: list of strings.
- uncertainties: list of strings.

We need to be concise and within token budget.
